content:
Choose the pair of elements $[(\mathrm{A})$ to $(\mathrm{F})]$ which:

(i) belong to the Chalcogen family

(ii) form monovalent oxides which give alkalies in aqueous solution

(iii) form stable monovalent anions

(A) $1 s^2 2 s^2 2 p^6 3 s^2 3 p^6 4 s^1$

(B) $1 s^2 2 s^2 2 p^5$

(C) $1 s^2 2 s^2 2 p^6 3 s^1$

(D) $1 s^2 2 s^2 2 p^6 3 s^2 3 p^5$

(E) $1 s^2 2 s^2 2 p^6 3 s^2 3 p^4$

(F) $1 s^2 2 s^2 2 p^4$

Given are $$\mathrm{H}_3 \mathrm{PO}_2, \mathrm{H}_3 \mathrm{PO}_3, \mathrm{H}_3 \mathrm{PO}_4$$ and $$\mathrm{H}_4 \mathrm{P}_2 \mathrm{O}_7$$. Which of the above oxacids results into two series of salts?
explanation: Phosphorus acid $$\left(\mathrm{H}_3 \mathrm{PO}_3\right)$$ is a diprotic acid. If forms two series of salts such as $$\mathrm{NaH}_2 \mathrm{PO}_3$$ and $$\mathrm{Na}_2 \mathrm{HPO}_3$$ but none of the type $$\mathrm{NaPO}_3$$ with $$\mathrm{NaOH}$$. Its structure is as

content: The order of acidic strength of boron trihalides is
explanation: Boron trihalides are Lewis acids. The back bonding determines its acidic strength, more is the $$\pi$$-back bonding, less is the acidity. Thus, due to better overlapping of orbitals it promotes better $$\pi$$-back bonding, hence $$\mathrm{BF}_3$$ is least acidic in nature. Thus, order of acidic strength is $$\mathrm{BF}_3<\mathrm{BCl}_3<\mathrm{BBr}_3 <\mathrm{BI}_3$$.

content: Give the correct order of acidity of
explanation:
Acidic strength of oxo-acids containing the same halogen are in the order:

HCl < HClO$$_2$$ < HClO$$_3$$ < HClO$$_4$$

This is because ClO$$_4^-$$ is the most stable due to dispersal of negative charge on four D-atoms

(O being more electromagnetic than Cl).

content: The cololur of CrO$$_4^{2-}$$ changes to Cr$$_2$$O$$_7^{2-}$$ is
explanation: The CrO$$_4^{2-}$$ (yellow) exist in acidic medium with pH = 6 and changes to orange in basic medium as Cr$$_2$$O$$_7^{2-}$$ with pH = 8.

content: Arrange the hydrides of group 15 in correct decreasing order of reducing nature.
explanation:
Trihydrides of group 15 elements are given on the basis of reducing character as follows:

BiH$$_3$$ > SbH$$_3$$ > AsH$$_3$$ > PH$$_3$$ > NH$$_3$$

The reducing character increases down the group because the bond strength of E-H bond decreases. As the size of element E down the group increases and thus, the bond dissociation energy decreases. This facilitates the dissociation of hydrogen atoms and hence, down the group, the reducing character increase.

content: When conc. H$$_2$$SO$$_4$$ is heated with P$$_2$$O$$_5$$, the acid is converted into

options.content: a mixture of sulphur dioxide and sulphur trioxide

explanation:
When conc. H$$_2$$SO$$_4$$ is heated with P$$_2$$O$$_5$$, the acid is converted into sulphur trioxide.

H$$_2$$SO$$_4$$ + P$$_2$$O$$_5$$ $$\to$$ SO$$_3$$ + 2HPO$$_3$$
